title: `links qr` — QR code generation
description: Implement `links qr` to generate a QR code for the link page URL. Output to terminal (ASCII) by default, with an option to save as PNG.

features: If url is omitted: use config.domain from links.yaml. If config.domain is also not set, print error: 'No URL specified and no domain set in links.yaml. Run: links qr <url>' and exit 1
--out <file>: save QR code as PNG to specified file path using qrcode package (qrcode.toFile())
Default (no --out): render QR code as ASCII/unicode blocks in the terminal using qrcode.toString() with type 'terminal'
Print the URL below the QR code so the user knows what it encodes
--link <label>: generate QR for a specific link's URL (looks up label in config) instead of the page URL

notes: The 'qrcode' npm package supports both terminal ASCII output and PNG file output. Use qrcode.toString(url, {type:'terminal'}) for terminal output and qrcode.toFile(path, url) for PNG.
